extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). Only one rule sets rec_drug_a: rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which fires when qt_prolongation AND hypertension AND low_ejection_fraction AND g6pd_deficiency AND peptic_ulcer AND bradycardia all hold. Only one rule sets rec_drug_b: rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50), which fires when qt_prolongation holds. Crucially, the conjunction for rec_drug_a includes qt_prolongation, so any input satisfying the rec_drug_a conjunction necessarily also satisfies the rec_drug_b trigger. Neither output has a competing rule that could set it false, so priorities, tie-breaking, and defaults are irrelevant — once both rules fire they both assign true and stay true. Setting the six conjunction inputs to true (with asthma/pregnancy/renal_impairment false, since those only affect rec_drug_c) yields rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. The property is violated.
